perturbation.original_text: You agree to provide the Funds, upon written request, the taxpayer identification number ("TIN"), if known, of any or all Shareholder(s) of an account and the amount, date, name or other identifier of any registered representative(s) associated with the Shareholder(s) or account (if known), and transaction type (purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange) of every purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange of Shares held through an account maintained by you during the period covered by the request.
perturbation.changed_text: You may provide the Funds, upon written request, the taxpayer identification number ("TIN"), if known, of any or all Shareholder(s) of an account, and transaction type (purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange) of every purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange of Shares held through an account maintained by you during the period covered by the request.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "agree to provide" to "may provide," the contract removes the *required* sharing of shareholder information to the Funds, weakening investor protection and compliance with SEC regulations. This creates a legal loophole.
perturbation.contradicted_law: SEC Rule 22c-2 under the Investment Company Act of 1940
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 22c-2 requires funds to have policies and procedures to prevent market timing, including the ability to obtain shareholder information. By making the provision of TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) and transaction information optional ('may provide' instead of 'agree to provide'), the agreement hinders the fund's ability to comply with Rule 22c-2, as it limits the availability of necessary information to monitor and prevent market timing activities.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 270.22c-2 - Redemption fees for redeemable securities. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 270.22c-2 - Redemption fees for redeemable securities. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 270.22c-2 Redemption fees for redeemable securities. (a) Redemption fee. It is unlawful for any fund issuing redeemable securities, its principal underwriter , or any dealer in such securities, to redeem a redeemable security issued by the fund within seven calendar days after the security was purchased, unless it complies with the following requirements: (1) Board determination. The fund 's board of directors, including a majority of directors who are not interested persons of the fund , must either: (i) Approve a redemption fee, in an amount (but no more than two percent of the value of shares redeemed) and on shares redeemed within a time period (but no less than seven calendar days), that in its judgment is necessary or appropriate to recoup for the fund the costs it may incur as a result of those redemptions or to otherwise eliminate or reduce so far as practicable any dilution of the value of the outstanding securities issued by the fund , the proceeds of which fee will be retained by the fund ; or (ii) Determine that imposition of a redemption fee is either not necessary or not appropriate. (2) Shareholder information. With respect to each financial intermediary that submits orders, itself or through its agent, to purchase or redeem shares directly to the fund , its principal underwriter or transfer agent, or to a registered clearing agency, the fund (or on the fund 's behalf, the principal underwriter or transfer agent) must either: (i) Enter into a shareholder information agreement with the financial intermediary (or its agent); or (ii) Prohibit the financial intermediary from purchasing in nominee name on behalf of other persons, securities issued by th
perturbation.justification: The change from "agree to provide" to "may provide" significantly weakens the obligation to share shareholder information. This contradicts the spirit of Rule 22c-2, as explained in the Law Explanation, which requires funds to have policies to prevent market timing, including obtaining shareholder information. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is irrelevant, Scraped Law Snippet 2 references 17 CFR § 270.22c-2, concerning redemption fees for redeemable securities. Although it doesn't directly state the requirement to provide shareholder information to prevent market timing, the Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation highlight the importance of this information for compliance with Rule 22c-2, making the optional provision a contradiction. The original text ensures compliance; the changed text creates a loophole.

perturbation.original_text: You agree to promptly and fully cooperate with any reasonable request made by the Fund to address market timing or excessive trading strategies identified by Nationwide in accordance with the applicable provisions of Rule 22c-2 and agree to restrict or prohibit further purchases or exchanges of Shares by a Shareholder that has been identified by such Fund as having engaged in transactions of the Fund's Shares (directly or indirectly through your account) that violate policies established by the Fund for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding Shares issued by the Fund.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to cooperate with any request made by the Fund to address market timing or excessive trading strategies. You may restrict or prohibit further purchases or exchanges of Shares by a Shareholder that has been identified by such Fund as having engaged in transactions of the Fund's Shares (directly or indirectly through your account) that violate policies established by the Fund for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding Shares issued by the Fund.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "promptly and fully" weakens the requirement to cooperate. Also, changing "agree to restrict or prohibit" to "may restrict or prohibit" makes restricting trading optional. This weakens the fund's ability to enforce its market timing policies and comply with SEC Rule 22c-2.
perturbation.contradicted_law: SEC Rule 22c-2 under the Investment Company Act of 1940
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 22c-2 mandates that funds must have the ability to restrict or prohibit further purchases or exchanges of shares by shareholders engaging in market timing activities. By making this action discretionary ('may restrict or prohibit'), the altered agreement does not guarantee the fund's ability to enforce its policies effectively and comply with the requirements of Rule 22c-2.

CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 270.22c-2 Redemption fees for redeemable securities. (a) Redemption fee. It is unlawful for any fund issuing redeemable securities, its principal underwriter , or any dealer in such securities, to redeem a redeemable security issued by the fund within seven calendar days after the security was purchased, unless it complies with the following requirements: (1) Board determination. The fund 's board of directors, including a majority of directors who are not interested persons of the fund , must either: (i) Approve a redemption fee, in an amount (but no more than two percent of the value of shares redeemed) and on shares redeemed within a time period (but no less than seven calendar days), that in its judgment is necessary or appropriate to recoup for the fund the costs it may incur as a result of those redemptions or to otherwise eliminate or reduce so far as practicable any dilution of the value of the outstanding securities issued by the fund , the proceeds of which fee will be retained by the fund ; or (ii) Determine that imposition of a redemption fee is either not necessary or not appropriate. (2) Shareholder information. With respect to each financial intermediary that submits orders, itself or through its agent, to purchase or redeem shares directly to the fund , its principal underwriter or transfer agent, or to a registered clearing agency, the fund (or on the fund 's behalf, the principal underwriter or transfer agent) must either: (i) Enter into a shareholder information agreement with the financial intermediary (or its agent); or (ii) Prohibit the financial intermediary from purchasing in nominee name on behalf of other persons, securities issued by th
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the fund's ability to enforce its market timing policies, potentially conflicting with the obligations under Rule 22c-2. The original text requires full cooperation and agreement to restrict or prohibit trading, while the changed text makes cooperation less stringent and restricting trading optional ('may restrict or prohibit'). Scraped Law Snippet 2 refers to 17 CFR § 270.22c-2, addressing redemption fees and the need for funds to eliminate or reduce dilution of value. By making the restriction discretionary, the altered agreement undermines the fund's ability to comply effectively with the requirements of Rule 22c-2 and ensure it can recoup costs or prevent dilution.

perturbation.original_text: Each party to this Agreement acknowledges, represents, and warrants that it has adopted and implemented an Anti-Money Laundering Program that complies and will continue to comply with all aspects and requirements of the ACT, the BSA, and all other applicable anti-money laundering laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party to this Agreement acknowledges that it has an Anti-Money Laundering Program. Each party intends to comply with the ACT, the BSA, and all other applicable anti-money laundering laws and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "represents, and warrants" and changing "complies and will continue to comply" to "intends to comply", the contract weakens the assurance that the parties are actively compliant with AML laws. This omission introduces legal risk related to compliance with the Bank Secrecy Act and USA Patriot Act.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), 31 U.S.C. § 5311 et seq.
perturbation.law_explanation: 31 U.S.C. § 5318(h) requires financial institutions to establish and maintain anti-money laundering programs, including ongoing compliance. By changing the language to indicate a mere 'intention' to comply, the agreement weakens the commitment to maintain an effective AML program, potentially violating the BSA's requirement for continuous and active compliance.

Compliance, exemptions, and summons authority (a) General Powers of Secretary .-The Secretary of the Treasury may (except under section 5315 of this title and regulations prescribed under section 5315)- (1) except as provided in subsections (b)(2) and (h)(4), delegate duti
perturbation.justification: The original contract text includes a representation and warranty that each party *complies* with AML laws and *will continue to comply*. The changed text replaces this with a statement that each party *intends to comply*. 31 U.S.C. § 5318(h) requires financial institutions to maintain anti-money laundering programs, including ongoing compliance. By changing the language to indicate a mere 'intention' to comply, the agreement weakens the commitment to maintain an effective AML program. Therefore, the change undermines the legal requirement for ongoing compliance, creating a legal contradiction.
